Name: More than 5 Global Administrators exist
Description:
This indicator checks for the presence of 5 or more Global Administrators. Please note: If a given user has both an active and eligible assignment for a given role, the user will appear twice in the results.

Required permissions

  • RoleManagement.Read.Directory
  • RoleManagement.Read.All
  • Directory.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Users that hold the Global Administrator role are the most privileged users in Entra ID. Organizations should limit the number of Global Administrators to less than five.

An attacker will find users with the Global Administrator role as high-value, and a compromised Global Administrator can lead to several attacks against the organization, that can include data exfiltration, lateral movement into other systems in Azure, multi-cloud, and on-premise systems, and can result in complete tenant compromise where an organization becomes locked out of their Entra tenant by the attacker.

References

Create an access review of Azure resource and Microsoft Entra roles in PIM - Microsoft Entra ID Governance | Microsoft Learn

Best practices for Microsoft Entra roles - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn

Manage emergency access admin accounts - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Found {0} Global Administrators in Azure AD.
Remediation:
Review the users assigned the Global Administrator role, and determine if the role assignment can be removed.


To review and manage role assignments, open the Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link), expand Identity, select Show more, expand Roles & admins, and select Roles & admins.

In the blade on the right, find the Global Administrator role. Select the role.

From this view you can remove users as necessary from the role. Organizations that have Entra ID P2 licensing available should use access reviews to manage the lifecycle of privileged users.

Organizations must ensure that any removal of the Global Administrator role does not impact the ability to manage their Entra tenant.
